Be approximately right rather than exactly wrong. — John Tukey
Let the future tell the truth and evaluate each one according to his work and accomplishments. The present is theirs; the future, for which I have really worked, is mine. — Nikola Tesla
The grass is greener where you water it. — Neil Barringham
Everything you can imagine is real. — Pablo Picasso
When I was 40, my doctor advised me that a man in his 40s shouldn't play tennis. I heeded his advice carefully and could hardly wait until I reached 50 to start again. — Hugo Black
Meanings are not determined by situations, but we determine ourselves by the meanings we give to situations. — Alfred Adler
The greater the impact you want to make, the greater your influence needs to be. — Lolly Daskal
Yesterday I dared to struggle. Today I dare to win. — Bernadette Devlin
Confidence comes from crossing thresholds. — Kamal Ravikant
Happiness depends upon ourselves. — Aristotle
When one does not have what one wants, one must want what one has. — Sigmund Freud
None knows the weight of another's burden. — George Herbert
It's not what you look at that matters, it's what you see. — Henry David Thoreau
When you reach the end of your rope, tie a knot in it and hang on. — Franklin D. Roosevelt
Walking with a friend in the dark is better than walking alone in the light. — Helen Keller
If you find yourself in a hole, the first thing to do is stop digging. — Will Rogers
We are all inclined to judge ourselves by our ideals; others, by their acts. — Harold Nicolson
We can either add to our character each day, or we can fritter away our energies in distractions. — Ming-Dao Deng
Friendship with oneself is all important because without it one cannot be friends with anybody else in the world. — Eleanor Roosevelt
If we open a quarrel between past and present, we shall find that we have lost the future. — Winston Churchill
The inherent vice of capitalism is the unequal sharing of blessings; the inherent virtue of socialism is the equal sharing of miseries. — Winston Churchill
Throw your heart over the fence and the rest will follow — Norman Vincent Peale
Wise people, even though all laws were abolished, would still lead the same life. — Aristophanes
I won't close down a business of subnormal profitability merely to add a fraction of a point to our corporate returns. I also feel it inappropriate for even an exceptionally profitable company to fund an operation once it appears to have unending losses in prospect. Adam Smith would disagree with my first proposition and Karl Marx would disagree with my second; the middle ground is the only position that leaves me comfortable. — Warren Buffett
Courage is the price that life exacts for granting peace. — Amelia Earhart
Everything you've ever wanted is on the other side of fear. — George Addair
In wisdom gathered over time I have found that every experience is a form of exploration. — Ansel Adams
The exercise of an extraordinary gift is the supremist pleasure in life. — Mark Twain
Keep your face always toward the sunshine -  shadows will fall behind you. — Walt Whitman
Be glad of life because it gives you the chance to love, to work, to play, and to look up at the stars. — Henry van Dyke Jr.
Life is essentially an endless series of problems. The solution to one problem is merely the creation of another. — Mark Manson
Falling down is not failure. Failure comes when you stay where you have fallen. — Socrates
In all chaos there is a cosmos, in all disorder a secret order. — Carl Jung
He who knows how to wait need make no concessions. — Sigmund Freud
Though no one can go back and make a brand new start, anyone can start from now and make a brand new ending. — Carl Bard
The universe is full of magical things, patiently waiting for our wits to grow sharper. — Eden Phillpotts
The beginning is always today. — Mary Wollstonecraft
Whenever people agree with me I always feel I must be wrong. — Oscar Wilde
Life is like a sewer. What you get out of it depends on what you put into it. — Tom Lehrer
Remember that sometimes not getting what you want is a wonderful stroke of luck. — Dalai Lama
Always seek out the seed of triumph in every adversity. — Og Mandino
Success is not the key to happiness. Happiness is the key to success. If you love what you are doing, you will be successful. — Albert Schweitzer
All that a man achieves and all that he fails to achieve is the direct result of his own thoughts. — James Allen
Character is doing what you don't want to do but know you should do. — Joyce Meyer
The four P's to success are pressure, persistence, perseverance, and prayer. — Steve Harvey
Cherish forever what makes you unique, cuz you're really a yawn if it goes. — Bette Midler
When you are talented you do it whatever it is until your fingers bleed or your eyes are ready to fall out of your head. — Stephen King
Beginning today, treat everyone you meet as if they were going to be dead by midnight. Your life will never be the same again. — Og Mandino
In the long run, the sharpest weapon of all is a kind and gentle spirit. — Anne Frank
Tomorrow is only found in the calendar of fools. — Og Mandino
Don't be afraid to see what you see. — Ronald Reagan
You can put lipstick on a pig. It's still a pig. — Barack Obama
Time is more valuable than money. You can get more money, but you cannot get more time. — Jim Rohn
Never give up, for that is just the place and time that the tide will turn. — Harriet Beecher Stowe
Give me six hours to chop down a tree and I will spend the first four sharpening the axe. — Abraham Lincoln
He is a wise man who does not grieve for the things which he has not but rejoices for those which he has. — Epictetus
In action a great heart is the chief qualification. In work, a great head. — Arthur Schopenhauer
Think how hard physics would be if particles could think. — Murray Gell-Mann
You can't get to a place that you don't believe exists. — Unknown
If I know what love is, it is because of you. — Hermann Hesse
Words have no power to impress the mind without the exquisite horror of their reality. — Edgar Allan Poe
The significance of life is living. — Jiddu Krishnamurti
Embody what you teach, and teach only what you have embodied. — Dan Millman
Life is about not knowing and then doing something anyway. — Mark Manson
I'm a great believer in luck and I find the harder I work, the more I have of it. — Thomas Jefferson
Never mistake motion for action. — Ernest Hemingway
Your imagination is your preview of life's coming attractions. — Albert Einstein
It's just a job. Grass grows, birds fly, waves pound the sand. I beat people up. — Muhammad Ali
The superior man is satisfied and composed; the mean man is always full of distress. — Confucius
If you only have a hammer, you tend to see every problem as a nail. — Abraham Maslow
Your success and happiness lie in you. — Helen Keller
Coming together is a beginning; keeping together is progress; working together is success. — Edward Everett Hale
True silence is the rest of the mind; it is to the spirit what sleep is to the body, nourishment and refreshment. — William Penn
Wise men make more opportunities than they find. — Francis Bacon
Those who are free of resentful thoughts surely find peace. — The Buddha
Be mindful. Be grateful. Be positive. Be true. Be kind. — Roy T. Bennett
Rejoicing in our joy, not suffering over our suffering, makes someone a friend. — Friedrich Nietzsche
People are so constituted that everybody would rather undertake what they see others do, whether they have an aptitude for it or not. — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
The smallest act of kindness is worth more than the grandest intention. — Oscar Wilde
The doctor of the future will give no medicine, but will interest her or his patients in the care of the human frame, in a proper diet, and in the cause and prevention of disease. — Thomas Edison
With pride, there are many curses. With humility, there come many blessings. — Ezra Taft Benson
When you discover your mission, you will feel its demand. It will fill you with enthusiasm and a burning desire to get to work on it. — W. Clement Stone
You are now, and you do become, what you think about. — Earl Nightingale
If you see yourself as prosperous, you will be. — Robert Collier
I'm selfish, impatient and a little insecure. I make mistakes, I am out of control and at times hard to handle. But if you can't handle me at my worst, then you sure as hell don't deserve me at my best. — Marilyn Monroe
You have to believe in yourself. — Sun Tzu
The most technologically efficient machine that man has ever invented is the book. — Northrop Frye
If you are depressed, you are living in the past. If you are anxious, you are living in the future. if you are at peace, you are living in the present. — Lao Tzu
Sometimes you have to travel a long way to find what is near. — Paulo Coelho
Our intention creates our reality. — Wayne Dyer
The Only Thing That Is Constant Is Change — Heraclitus
A really great talent finds its happiness in execution. — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

If the stars should appear but one night every thousand years how man would marvel and adore. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Ignorance and bungling with love are better than wisdom and skill without. — Henry David Thoreau
Trust yourself. You know more than you think you do. — Benjamin Spock
The joy of life comes from our encounters with new experiences. — Christopher McCandless
A heart well prepared for adversity in bad times hopes, and in good times fears for a change in fortune. — Horace
There is never enough time to do everything, but there is always enough time to do the most important thing. — Brian Tracy
All life is an experiment. The more experiments you make, the better. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
You're only given a little spark of madness. You mustn't lose it. — Robin Williams
Work for something because it is good, not just because it stands a chance to succeed. — Václav Havel
My guiding principle is this: Guilt is never to be doubted.  — Franz Kafka
The only Zen you find on the tops of mountains is the Zen you bring up there. — Robert M. Pirsig
Being deeply loved by someone gives you strength, while loving someone deeply gives you courage.  — Lao Tzu
A wise man makes his own decisions, but an ignorant man mindlessly follows the crowd. — Chinese Proverb
Men in general judge more from appearances than from reality. All men have eyes, but few have the gift of penetration. — Niccolò Machiavelli
No experience is a cause of success or failure. We do not suffer from the shock of our experiences, so-called trauma - but we make out of them just what suits our purposes. — Alfred Adler
He who controls others may be powerful, but he who has mastered himself is mightier still. — Laozi
Our virtues and our failings are inseparable, like force and matter. When they separate, man is no more. — Nikola Tesla
Smile, breathe and go slowly. — Thích Nhất Hạnh
Take the attitude of a student. Never be too big to ask questions. Never know too much to learn something new. — Og Mandino
The world is round and the place which may seem like the end may also be the beginning. — Ivy Baker Priest
We are shaped and fashioned by what we love. — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
What is necessary to change a person is to change his awareness of himself. — Abraham Maslow
We are either progressing or retrograding all the while. There is no such thing as remaining stationary in this life. — James Freeman Clarke
I love my past. I love my present. I'm not ashamed of what I've had, and I'm not sad because I have it no longer. — Colette
Good thoughts are no better than good dreams, unless they be executed. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
The greatest pleasure I know is to do a good action by stealth, and to have it found out by accident. — Charles Lamb
No matter how qualified or deserving we are, we will never reach a better life until we can imagine it for ourselves and allow ourselves to have it. — Richard Bach
Everyone has a sense of humor. If you don't laugh at jokes, you probably laugh at opinions. — Criss Jami
A friend may well be reckoned the masterpiece of nature. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Life is love and love is life. — Elvis Presley
You always succeed in producing a result. — Tony Robbins
Quality is never an accident; it is always the result of intelligent effort. — John Ruskin
It is very easy to forgive others their mistakes; it takes more grit to forgive them for having witnessed your own. — Jessamyn West
If there is no struggle, there is no progress. — Frederick Douglass
A friend is someone who gives you total freedom to be yourself. — Jim Morrison
The first half of life is devoted to forming a healthy ego, the second half is going inward and letting go of it. — Carl Jung
Decide what your truth is. Then live it. — Kamal Ravikant
All that we see or seem is but a dream within a dream. — Edgar Allan Poe
I have learned that friendship isn't about who you've known the longest, it's about who came and never left your side. — Yolanda Hadid
Our most intimate friend is not he to whom we show the worst, but the best of our nature. — Nathaniel Hawthorne
Life has no limitations except the ones you make. — Les Brown
Strong people make as many mistakes as weak people. Difference is that strong people admit their mistakes, laugh at them, learn from them. That is how they become strong. — Richard Needham
It's important to know that words don't move mountains. Work, exacting work moves mountains. — Danilo Dolci
Never make friends with people who are above or below you in status. Such friendships will never give you any happiness. — Chanakya

Experience is a comb which nature gives us when we are bald.  — Chinese Proverb
You will never be happy if you continue to search for what happiness consists of. You will never live if you are looking for the meaning of life. — Albert Camus

Thinking is difficult, that's why most people judge. — Carl Jung
The dreamers are the saviors of the worlds. — James Allen

To be wrong is nothing unless you continue to remember it. — Confucius
Every moment of happiness requires a great amount of Ignorance — Honore de Balzac
I destroy my enemies when I make them my friends. — Abraham Lincoln
A subtle thought that is in error may yet give rise to fruitful inquiry that can establish truths of great value. — Isaac Asimov
The aim of life is self-development. To realize one's nature perfectly - that is what each of us is here for. — Oscar Wilde
Opportunity is missed by most because it is dressed in overalls and looks like work. — Thomas Edison
I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work. — Thomas Edison

People may doubt what you say, but they will believe what you do. — Lewis Cass
Nothing we see or hear is perfect. But right there in the imperfection is perfect reality. — Shunryu Suzuki
Whenever you do a thing, act as if all the world were watching. — Thomas Jefferson

It is not fair to ask of others what you are not willing to do yourself. — Eleanor Roosevelt

Don't just sit there. Do something. The answers will follow. — Mark Manson
Man's character is his fate. — Heraclitus

To every action there is always opposed an equal reaction. — Isaac Newton

Death walks faster than the wind and never returns what he has taken. — Hans Christian Andersen

An invincible determination can accomplish almost anything and in this lies the great distinction between great men and little men. — Thomas Fuller

Care about what other people think and you will always be their prisoner. — Lao Tzu
From wonder into wonder existence opens. — Laozi

All fixed set patterns are incapable of adaptability or pliability. The truth is outside of all fixed patterns. — Bruce Lee
Nature gave us one tongue and two ears so we could hear twice as much as we speak. — Epictetus

Where violence reigns, reason is weak. — Nicolas Chamfort
There is no truth. There is only perception. — Gustave Flaubert

The ladder of success is never crowded at the top. — Napoleon Hill

Give so much away people insist on paying you. — Jack Butcher
Nothing can dim the light which shines from within. — Maya Angelou
When people are like each other they tend to like each other. — Tony Robbins

Every man takes the limits of his own field of vision for the limits of the world. — Arthur Schopenhauer

It is easy to discover what another has discovered before. — Christopher Columbus

They blame those who remain silent, they blame those who speak much, they blame those who speak in moderation. There is none in the world who is not blamed. — The Buddha

Success is often achieved by those who don't know that failure is inevitable. — Coco Chanel

Success is getting what you want, happiness is wanting what you get. — W.P. Kinsella

What you seek is seeking you. — Rumi

The hours of folly are measured by the clock; but of wisdom, no clock can measure. — William Blake

Let my soul smile through my heart and my heart smile through my eyes, that I may scatter rich smiles in sad hearts.  — Paramahansa Yogananda
The two most powerful warriors are patience and time. — Leo Tolstoy
A true friend freely, advises justly, assists readily, adventures boldly, takes all patiently, defends courageously, and continues a friend unchangeably. — William C. Menninger
Neatness begets order; but from order to taste there is the same difference as from taste to genius, or from love to friendship. — Johann Kaspar Lavater
Don't smother each other. No one can grow in the shade. — Leo Buscaglia
Learning never exhausts the mind. — Leonardo da Vinci
Everything that is done in the world is done by hope. — Martin Luther
He who talks more is sooner exhausted. — Laozi

The World is my country, all mankind are my brethren, and to do good is my religion. — Thomas Paine
I'd rather regret the things that I have done than the things that I have not done. — Lucille Ball
The superior man acts before he speaks, and afterwards speaks according to his action. — Confucius
Older men declare war. But it is youth that must fight and die. — Herbert Hoover
Honesty is the first chapter in the book of wisdom. — Thomas Jefferson

Keep smiling, because life is a beautiful thing and there's so much to smile about. — Marilyn Monroe

We may encounter many defeats, but we must not be defeated. — Maya Angelou
Between saying and doing, many a pair of shoes is worn out. — Iris Murdoch

Everybody by your side ain't on your side. — Steve Harvey

Three things in human life are important. The first is to be kind. The second is to be kind. The third is to be kind. — Henry James
Don't learn to do, but learn in doing. — Samuel Butler
Do not take life too seriously. You will never get out of it alive. — Elbert Hubbard
Our life isn't how much we can take out, but how much we can put in.   — Estee Lauder
Things turn out best for the people who make the best of the way things turn out. — John Wooden
The more you like yourself, the less you are like anyone else, which makes you unique. — Walt Disney
No one has a finer command of language than the person who keeps his mouth shut. — Sam Rayburn
Life is a progress, and not a station. — Ralph Waldo Emerson

Life is a traveling to the edge of knowledge, then a leap taken. — D. H. Lawrence
Youth ages, immaturity is outgrown, ignorance can be educated, and drunkenness sobered, but stupid lasts forever. — Aristophanes
I do not seek. I find. — Pablo Picasso
Talk to someone about themselves and they'll listen for hours. — Dale Carnegie
We must not allow ourselves to become like the system we oppose. — Desmond Tutu
Treat everyone with politeness and kindness, not because they are nice, but because you are. — Roy T. Bennett
Your business is not to 'get somewhere' - it is to be here. — Dan Millman

Presents are made for the pleasure of who gives them, not the merits of who receives them. — Carlos Ruiz Zafon
The quality, not the longevity, of one's life is what is important. — Martin Luther King, Jr.
Throughout this life, you can never be certain of living long enough to take another breath. — Zen Proverb
Whether you think you can or you think you can't, you are right. — Henry Ford
Love is the attempt to form a friendship inspired by beauty. — Cicero
To understand the heart and mind of a person, look not at what he has already achieved, but at what he aspires to do. — Kahlil Gibran
If you spend too much time thinking about a thing, you'll never get it done. — Bruce Lee
Everything great that has ever happened to humanity has begun as a single thought in someone's mind. — Yanni
You are free, and that is why you are lost. — Franz Kafka
To change one's life, start immediately, do it flamboyantly, no exceptions. — William James
Adversity is the tempering of one's mettle. Without it, we cannot know any true meaning in our accomplishments. — Ming-Dao Deng
To be what we are, and to become what we are capable of becoming, is the only end of life. — Robert Louis Stevenson
If we look at the world with a love of life, the world will reveal its beauty to us. — Daisaku Ikeda
Make the most of yourself, for that is all there is of you. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Not all those who wander are lost. — J. R. R. Tolkien
Opportunities multiply as they are seized. — Sun Tzu
Freedom is not worth having if it does not connote freedom to err. — Mahatma Gandhi
If you think adventure is dangerous, try routine; it is lethal. — Paulo Coelho
He suffers more than necessary, who suffers before it is necessary.   — Seneca
When you see a man of worth, think of how you may emulate him. When you see one who is unworthy, examine yourself. — Confucius
Life is what happens to you while you're busy making other plans. — John Lennon
Unless a man is master of his soul, all other kinds of mastery amount to little. — Theodore Roosevelt
Those who dare to fail miserably can achieve greatly. — John F. Kennedy
Life is a balance of holding on and letting go. — Rumi
Not every difficult and dangerous thing is suitable for training, but only that which is conducive to success in achieving the object of our effort. — Epictetus
The best thing in every noble dream is the dreamer... — Moncure D. Conway
You can have results or excuses, but not both. — Arnold Schwarzenegger
Good advice is always certain to be ignored, but that's no reason not to give it. — Agatha Christie
Skill to do comes of doing. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
You already have every characteristic necessary for success if you recognize, claim, develop and use them. — Zig Ziglar
In the middle of every difficulty lies opportunity. — Albert Einstein
O, what a tangled web we weave when first we practice to deceive! — Walter Scott
Life becomes easier and more beautiful when we can see the good in other people. — Roy T. Bennett
Not all of us can do great things. But we can do small things with great love. — Mother Teresa
No grand idea was ever born in a conference, but a lot of foolish ideas have died there. — F. Scott Fitzgerald
When I dare to be powerful, to use my strength in the service of my vision, then it becomes less and less important whether I am afraid. — Audre Lorde
Be willing to be a beginner every single morning. — Meister Eckhart
Be thankful for what you have; you'll end up having more. If you concentrate on what you don't have, you will never, ever have enough.  — Oprah Winfrey
Even the smallest person can change the course of the future. — J.R.R. Tolkien
The first duty of a human being is to assume the right functional relationship to society - more briefly, to find your real job, and do it. — Charlotte Perkins Gilman
If we learn to open our hearts, anyone, including the people who drive us crazy, can be our teacher. — Pema Chodron
Blessed are the hearts that can bend; they shall never be broken. — Albert Camus
We are masters of the unsaid words, but slaves of those we let slip out. — Winston Churchill
Never waste a minute of your precious life thinking about people you don't like. — Celestine Chua
Peace is not the absence of conflict, but the ability to cope with it. — Unknown
He who angers you conquers you. — Elizabeth Kenny
When you get up in the morning, you have two choices - either to be happy or to be unhappy. Just choose to be happy. — Norman Vincent Peale
The biggest room in the world is room for improvement. — Helmut Schmidt
True it is, we only hate those whom we do not know. — Andrew Carnegie
It doesn't matter how much you want. What really matters is how much you want it. — Ralph Marston
Mastery is not a function of genius or talent, it is a function of time and intense focus applied to a particular field of knowledge. — Robert Greene
The greatest gift of life is friendship, and I have received it. — Hubert Humphrey
It is a common experience that a problem difficult at night is resolved in the morning after the committee of sleep has worked on it. — John Steinbeck
Maybe you are searching among the branches, for what only appears in the roots. — Rumi
In the depth of winter, I finally learned that there was within me an invincible summer. — Albert Camus
Work out your own salvation. Do not depend on others. — Buddha
A needle is not sharp at both ends.  — Chinese Proverb
It is during our darkest moments that we must focus to see the light. — Aristotle
Without the rain there would be no rainbow. — Gilbert Chesterton
If you truly love Nature, you will find beauty everywhere.  — Vincent van Gogh
Real loss is only possible when you love something more than you love yourself. — Robin Williams
Some of the greater things in life are unseen; that's why you close your eyes when you kiss, cry, or dream. — Unknown
By words the mind is winged. — Aristophanes
The greatest mistake you can make in life is to be continually fearing you will make one. — Elbert Hubbard
Cry. Forgive. Learn. Move on. Let your tears water the seeds of your future happiness. — Steve Maraboli
He who knows that enough is enough will always have enough. — Laozi
The happiest people in the world are those who feel absolutely terrific about themselves. — Brian Tracy
Technology has to be invented or adopted. — Jared Diamond
The wise warrior avoids the battle. — Sun Tzu
Cherish your visions and your dreams as they are the children of your soul; the blueprints of your ultimate achievements. — Napoleon Hill
Don't be afraid to stand for what you believe in, even if that means standing alone. — Unknown
Leaders aren't born they are made. And they are made just like anything else, through hard work. And that's the price we'll have to pay to achieve that goal, or any goal. — Vince Lombardi
Even if I knew that tomorrow the world would go to pieces, I would still plant my apple tree.  — Martin Luther
I'm not afraid of death, but I'm in no hurry to die. — Stephen Hawking
There is nothing quite so tragic as a young cynic. — Maya Angelou
If you hang out with chickens, you're going to cluck and if you hang out with eagles, you're going to fly. — Steve Maraboli
A man, as a general rule, owes very little to what he is born with - a man is what he makes of himself. — Alexander Graham Bell
Business, you know, may bring you money, but friendship hardly ever does. — Jane Austen
If ever you feel like an animal among men, be a lion. — Criss Jami
Know, first, who you are, and then adorn yourself accordingly. — Epictetus
I try more and more to be myself, caring relatively little whether people approve or disapprove. — Vincent van Gogh
Confuse them with your silence and amaze them with your actions. — Unknown
People are doing the best that they can from their own level of consciousness. — Deepak Chopra
No great discovery was ever made without a bold guess.  — Isaac Newton
Write your plans in pencil and give God the eraser. — Paulo Coelho
Consider the rights of others before your own feelings, and the feelings of others before your own rights. — John Wooden
Worry is a waste of emotional reserve. — Ayn Rand
He who is taught to live upon little owes more to his father's wisdom than he who has a great deal left him does to his father's care. — William C. Menninger
A man is great by deeds, not by birth. — Chanakya
Get mad, then get over it. — Colin Powell
With the realization of one's own potential and self-confidence in one's ability, one can build a better world. — Dalai Lama
We suffer more often in imagination than in reality. — Seneca
It is one of the severest tests of friendship to tell your friend his faults. So to love a man that you cannot bear to see a stain upon him, and to speak painful truth through loving words, that is friendship. — Henry Ward Beecher
Be as fluid as water, do not give your enemies anything solid to attack. — Robert Greene
The idea of ourselves is our escape from the fact of what we really are. — Jiddu Krishnamurti
Change your life today. Don't gamble on the future, act now, without delay. — Simone de Beauvoir
This is the final test of a gentleman: his respect for those who can be of no possible value to him. — William Lyon Phelps
Happiness is pretty simple: someone to love, something to do, something to look forward to. — Rita Mae Brown
It has never been my object to record my dreams, just to realize them. — Man Ray
We gain the strength of the temptation we resist. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
For it was not into my ear you whispered, but into my heart. It was not my lips you kissed, but my soul. — Judy Garland
Remember the entrance to the sanctuary is inside you. — Rumi
The greatest achievement of humanity is not its works of art, science, or technology, but the recognition of its own dysfunction. — Eckhart Tolle
There is no path to Peace. Peace is the path. — Dan Millman
We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give. — Unknown
Happiness is a gift and the trick is not to expect it, but to delight in it when it comes. — Charles Dickens
One of the most beautiful qualities of true friendship is to understand and to be understood. — Seneca the Younger
He who knows, does not speak. He who speaks, does not know. — Laozi
To be able to give away riches is mandatory if you wish to possess them. This is the only way that you will be truly rich. — Muhammad Ali
Better to light a candle than to curse the darkness.  — Chinese Proverb
Do not fear failure but rather fear not trying. — Roy T. Bennett
It's not the men in your life that matters, it's the life in your men. — Mae West
Just throw away all thoughts of imaginary things, and stand firm in that which you are. — Kabir
Value your friendship. Value your relationships. — Barbara Bush

I have no methods; all I do is accept people as they are. — Joan Rivers
Whoso loves, believes the impossible. — Elizabeth Browning
Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom. — Aristotle
Intelligent men are cruel. Stupid men are monstrously cruel. — Jack London
Everyone you meet is fighting a battle you know nothing about. Be kind. Always. — Robin Williams
From a certain point onward there is no longer any turning back. That is the point that must be reached. — Franz Kafka
Never mistake activity for achievement. — John Wooden
There are two ways to slide easily through life: to believe everything or to doubt everything; both ways save us from thinking. — Alfred Korzybski
I cannot always control what goes on outside. But I can always control what goes on inside. — Wayne Dyer
Anything becomes interesting if you look at it long enough.. — Gustave Flaubert
Most of our pocket wisdom is conceived for the use of mediocre people, to discourage them from ambitious attempts, and generally console them in their mediocrity. — Robert Louis Stevenson
In order to succeed, we must first believe that we can. — Nikos Kazantzakis
Confidence breeds beauty.   — Estee Lauder
To be happy is to be able to become aware of oneself without fright. — Walter Benjamin
If you only knew the magnificence of the 3, 6, and 9, then you would have a key to the universe. — Nikola Tesla
The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams. — Eleanor Roosevelt
You can stand tall without standing on someone. You can be a victor without having victims. — Harriet Woods
The less you open your heart to others, the more your heart suffers. — Deepak Chopra
What we dwell on is who we become. — Oprah Winfrey
Something opens our wings. Something makes boredom and hurt disappear. Someone fills the cup in front of us: We taste only sacredness. — Rumi
When a man is penalized for honesty he learns to lie. — Criss Jami
Do not worry about your difficulties in Mathematics. I can assure you mine are still greater. — Albert Einstein
Trust, but verify. — Ronald Reagan
We can spend our whole lives escaping from the monsters of our minds. — Pema Chodron
Sooner or later, those who win are those who think they can. — Richard Bach
You were not born a winner, and you were not born a loser. You are what you make yourself be. — Lou Holtz
He who possesses most must be most afraid of loss. — Leonardo da Vinci
Courage is fear that has said its prayers and decided to go forward anyway. — Joyce Meyer
You can't choose up sides on a round world. — Wayne Dyer
It is best to begin at the beginning. — Amelia Earhart
The awareness of our own strength makes us modest. — Paul Cézanne
Normal is the average of deviance. — Rita Mae Brown
It is with our passions, as it is with fire and water, they are good servants but bad masters. — Aesop
One who gains strength by overcoming obstacles possesses the only strength which can overcome adversity. — Albert Schweitzer
Computers are useless. They can only give you answers. — Pablo Picasso
An empty head is not really empty; it is stuffed with rubbish. Hence the difficulty of forcing anything into an empty head. — Eric Hoffer
Love is the only force capable of transforming an enemy into a friend. — Martin Luther King Jr.
Three things cannot be long hidden: the sun, the moon, and the truth. — The Buddha
Do what you can. Want what you have. Be who you are. — Forrest Church
A leader leads by example, not by force. — Sun Tzu
You use a glass mirror to see your face; you use works of art to see your soul. — George Bernard Shaw
Friendship always benefits; love sometimes injures. — Seneca the Younger
I know that inner wisdom is more precious than wealth. The more you spend it, the more you gain. — Oprah Winfrey
Don't spend time beating on a wall, hoping to transform it into a door. — Coco Chanel
Wisdom begins in wonder. — Socrates
Rest when you're weary. Refresh and renew yourself, your body, your mind, your spirit. Then get back to work. — Ralph Marston
The world makes way for the man who knows where he is going. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Power is not what we do but what we do not - hasty and unwise actions that we repeat every day and which ultimately bring us into trouble. — Robert Greene
Make peace within, and there will be no one who can overcome you. And no one you will wish to overcome. — Dan Millman
Learn to value yourself, which means: fight for your happiness. — Ayn Rand
Ever tried. Ever failed. No matter. Try Again. Fail again. Fail better.  — Samuel Beckett

To belittle, you have to be little. — Kahlil Gibran
There is nothing like looking if you want to find something. — J.R.R. Tolkien
Family not only needs to consist of merely those whom we share blood, but also of those whom we'd give blood. — Charles Dickens
Think in the morning. Act in the noon. Eat in the evening. Sleep in the night. — William Blake
The minute you settle for less than you deserve, you get even less than you settled for. — Maureen Dowd
Where your talents and the needs of the world cross, there lies your vocation. — Aristotle
Just try to be the best you can be; never cease trying to be the best you can be. That's in your power. — John Wooden
You will face many defeats in life, but never let yourself be defeated. — Maya Angelou
Yesterday is history, tomorrow is a mystery, today is God's gift, that's why we call it the present. — Joan Rivers
There is a wisdom of the head, and a wisdom of the heart. — Charles Dickens
Rather than waste time and energy worrying, use that time and energy to constructively deal with whatever is causing the worry. — Celestine Chua

Love is not about possession. Love is about appreciation. — Osho
Either write something worth reading or do something worth writing. — Benjamin Franklin
Identify your problems, but give your power and energy to solutions. — Tony Robbins
Each man has his own vocation; his talent is his call. There is one direction in which all space is open to him. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Muddy water is best cleared by leaving it alone. — Alan Watts
Begin now to be what you will be hereafter. — St. Jerome
Changing minds is harder than finding people who already agree. — Jack Butcher

Without hard work, nothing grows but weeds. — Gordon Hinckley
Truth is like most opinions - best unexpressed. — Kenneth Branagh
Give more than you think you can, trusting that you are richer than you think. — Jon Kabat-Zinn
People don't care how much you know until they know how much you care. — Theodore Roosevelt
You teach best what you most need to learn. — Richard Bach
You can't cross the sea merely by standing and staring at the water. — Rabindranath Tagore

There is no retirement for an artist, it's your way of living so there is no end to it. — Henry Moore
Our anxiety does not empty tomorrow of its sorrows, but only empties today of its strengths. — Charles Spurgeon
True wisdom comes to each of us when we realize how little we understand about life, ourselves, and the world around us. — Isocrates
A mountain never yields to the wind no matter how strong it is. — Zen Proverb
If you want something then you lose everything. If you don't want anything then you already have everything. — Seungsahn
Everything will be ok in the end. If it's not ok, it's not the end. — John Lennon
I enjoy life when things are happening. I don't care if it's good things or bad things. That means you're alive. — Joan Rivers
Without the confidence, nothing can be accomplished. — Sathya Sai Baba
You find peace not by rearranging the circumstances of your life, but by realizing who you are at the deepest level. — Eckhart Tolle
Be slow of tongue and quick of eye. — Miguel de Cervantes
Passion is born deaf and dumb. — Honore de Balzac
Everyone has an invisible sign hanging from their neck saying, 'Make me feel important.' Never forget this message when working with people. — Mary Kay Ash
The way you see people is the way you treat them, and the way you treat them is what they become. — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
Anyone who wants to achieve a dream must stay strong, focused and steady. — Estee Lauder
The most important thing in communication is to hear what isn't being said. — Peter Drucker
Always be yourself, express yourself, have faith in yourself, do not go out and look for a successful personality and duplicate it. — Bruce Lee
Some people are born on third base and go through life thinking they hit a triple. — Barry Switzer
There is nothing like returning to a place that remains unchanged to find the ways in which you yourself have altered. — Nelson Mandela

A hero is an ordinary person doing things in an extra ordinary way. — Christopher Reeve
Adults are just children who earn money. — Kenneth Branagh

We must be free not because we claim freedom, but because we practice it. — William Faulkner
Don't wait. The time will never be just right. — Napoleon Hill
Often we pass beside happiness without seeing it, without looking at it, or even if we have seen and looked at it, without recognizing it. — Alexandre Dumas
Deeds will not be less valiant because they are upraised. — J.R.R. Tolkien
All wealth is the product of labor. — John Locke
You cannot find peace avoiding life. — Virginia Woolf
The wisdom of the wise, and the experience of ages, may be preserved by quotation. — Isaac D'Israeli

If I were two-faced, would I be wearing this one? — Abraham Lincoln
Every great advance in science has issued from a new audacity of the imagination. — John Dewey
Everything that happens as it should, and if you observe carefully, you will find this to be so. — Marcus Aurelius

Respect was invented to cover the empty place where love should be. — Leo Tolstoy
Who looks outside, dreams, who looks inside, awakes. — Carl Jung
It is with words as with sunbeams. The more they are condensed, the deeper they burn. — Robert Southey
Don't let the fear of losing be greater than the excitement of winning. — Robert Kiyosaki

Be who you are and say what you feel because those who mind don't matter and those who matter don't mind. — Dr. Seuss
You cannot find peace by avoiding life. — Virginia Woolf
If we know how to adapt, we end up being superior. — Ming-Dao Deng
Unexpressed emotions will never die. They are buried alive and will come forth later in uglier ways. — Sigmund Freud
Great acts are made up of small deeds. — Laozi
Impossibilities are merely things which we have not yet learned. — Charles W. Chesnutt
In order to be irreplaceable one must always be different. — Coco Chanel

We will now discuss in a little more detail the Struggle for Existence. — Charles Darwin
If you try, you might get exactly what you want. If you don't, you don't. — Unknown

In three words I can sum up everything I've learned about life: it goes on. — Robert Frost
No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted. — Aesop
Nothing external to you has any power over you. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Courage is going from failure to failure without losing enthusiasm. — Winston Churchill
Rewards and punishment is the lowest form of education. — Zhuangzi
Everyone thinks of changing the world, but no one thinks of changing himself. — Leo Tolstoy
There are no strangers here; Only friends you haven't yet met. — William Butler Yeats
In the sky there are no tracks. Outside there is no recluse. There are no conditioned things that are eternal. There is no instability in the Buddhas. — The Buddha
The happiness that is genuinely satisfying is accompanied by the fullest exercise of our faculties and the fullest realization of the world in which we live. — Bertrand Russell
A tree is known by its fruit; a man by his deeds. A good deed is never lost; he who sows courtesy reaps friendship, and he who plants kindness gathers love. — Basil of Caesarea
Anger begins with folly, and ends with repentance.  — Beverly Sills
Absence makes the heart grow fonder. — Eleanor Roosevelt
Beauty is not in the face; beauty is a light in the heart. — Kahlil Gibran
To affect the quality of the day, that is the highest of arts. — Henry David Thoreau
There is nothing happens to any person but what was in his power to go through with. — Marcus Aurelius
Always forgive your enemies - nothing annoys them so much.  — Oscar Wilde
Experience is not what happens to a man. It is what a man does with what happens to him. — Aldous Huxley
Genius is patience. — Isaac Newton
Words used to attract the dull of wit are not to be relied on. — Huang Po
If a man knows not to which port he sails, no wind is favorable.   — Seneca
Curiosity is the most powerful thing you own. Imagination is a force that can actually manifest a reality. — James Cameron
When you're curious, you find lots of interesting things to do. — Walt Disney
To climb steep hills requires a slow pace at first. — William Shakespeare
The more one judges, the less one loves. — Honore de Balzac
Conditions fall apart. Persist with diligence. — The Buddha
Belief consists in accepting the affirmations of the soul; Unbelief, in denying them. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Friendship needs no words - it is solitude delivered from the anguish of loneliness. — Dag Hammarskjöld
Swim upstream. Go the other way. Ignore the conventional wisdom. — Sam Walton
A business that makes nothing but money is a poor business. — Henry Ford
Irrigators channel waters; fletchers straighten arrows; carpenters bend wood; the wise master themselves. — The Buddha
Giving is the secret of a healthy life. Not necessarily money, but whatever a person has of encouragement, sympathy and understanding. — John D. Rockefeller
A man is not old as long as he is seeking something. — Edmond Rostand
Remember where you came from, where you're going, and why you created this mess you got yourself into in the first place. — Richard Bach

Great things happen to those who don't stop believing, trying, learning, and being grateful. — Roy T. Bennett
Gratitude is not only the greatest of virtues, but the parent of all the others. — Cicero
Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated. — Confucius
Applause is a receipt, not a bill. — Dale Carnegie
Technology is the campfire around which we tell our stories. — Laurie Anderson
A woman unsatisfied must have luxuries. But a woman who loves a man would sleep on a board. — D. H. Lawrence
The fewer rules a coach has, the fewer rules there are for players to break. — John Madden
It is better to fail in originality than to succeed in imitation. — Herman Melville
The power of intuitive understanding will protect you from harm until the end of your days. — Laozi
God has given you one face, and you make yourself another. — William Shakespeare
The friendship that can cease has never been real.  — St. Jerome

What happens is not as important as how you react to what happens. — Ellen Glasgow
We are all born ignorant, but one must work hard to remain stupid. — Benjamin Franklin
I decided that it was not wisdom that enabled poets to write their poetry, but a kind of instinct or inspiration, such as you find in seers and prophets who deliver all their sublime messages without knowing in the least what they mean. — Isocrates
Happiness cannot be travelled to, owned, earned, worn or consumed. Happiness is the spiritual experience of living every minute with love, grace and gratitude. — Denis Waitley
Not how long, but how well you have lived is the main thing. — Seneca
The ballot is stronger than the bullet. — Abraham Lincoln
Where ever you are, you are one with the clouds and one with the sun and the stars you see. You are one with everything. — Shunryu Suzuki
Life is a question and how we live it is our answer. — Gary Keller
Sometimes things become possible if we want them bad enough. — T.S. Eliot
The superior man understands what is right; the inferior man understands what will sell. — Confucius
Humor isn't for everyone. It's only for people who want to have fun, enjoy life, and feel alive. — Anne Wilson Schaef
The wisdom of life consists in the elimination of the nonessentials. — Lin Yutang
Friendship is an arrangement by which we undertake to exchange small favors for big ones. — Montesquieu
Love isn't something you find. Love is something that finds you. — Loretta Young
Big people don't make people feel small. — Robin Sharma
Friendship is like money, easier made than kept. — Samuel Butler
He who learns must suffer. And even in our sleep pain that cannot forget falls drop by drop upon the heart, and in our own despair, against our will, comes wisdom to us by the awful grace of God. — Aeschylus
